entities.name: Mother Baker
entities.description: A woman identified as a famous witch in Kent, accused of using waxen images to harm others.

entities.name: Ovid
entities.description: Roman poet, cited as a 'poetical witch' who claims to break vipers' jaws with words.

entities.name: Serpents and Snakes
entities.description: Creatures often the subject of charms and enchantments in the text, discussed as being resistant to magic or susceptible to susceptibility.

entities.name: Horace
entities.description: Roman poet, cited to mock or impugn the power of witches.
